description: Twelve pipeline environment gotchas documented during pipeline development on Google Colab Pro. Each entry lists the symptom, root cause, and the fix that was locked into the pipeline.
environment: Google Colab Pro, T4 GPU runtime, high-RAM, Python 3.10

gotchas.symptom: STAR alignment produced junction records that did not match recount3-processed samples for the same input.
gotchas.root_cause: Default STAR parameters differ from the Monorail-external pipeline used by recount3.
gotchas.fix: Locked Monorail-canonical STAR parameters (see reference_versions.md). Amendment v5.1.

gotchas.symptom: Salmon transcript-level quantifications were incompatible with LeafCutter's junction-count input requirement.
gotchas.root_cause: LeafCutter operates on split-read alignments at the junction level, not on transcript-level abundance.
gotchas.fix: Dropped Salmon from the pipeline. Locked five persistent per-sample outputs (BAM, SJ.out.tab, ReadsPerGene.out.tab, regtools BED, arcasHLA JSON). Amendment v5.2.

gotchas.symptom: regtools v1.0.0 junction BED output contained 100 percent strand-missing records when paired with STAR Unsorted BAM output.
gotchas.root_cause: regtools infers strand from the XS SAM tag, which STAR emits only for canonical splice junctions in Unsorted BAM output.
gotchas.fix: Switched to STAR SJ.out.tab as the direct junction source, with GENCODE v43 GTF overlap for strand annotation. Amendment v5.4.4.

gotchas.symptom: The reference R and Stan LeafCutter implementation failed to compile on the Colab Pro environment despite multiple attempts across sessions.
gotchas.root_cause: Stan version incompatibility with the R packages available in Colab's default R environment; unresolvable within Colab's constrained package management.
gotchas.fix: Locked LeafCutter-DS as a Python and pyro re-implementation. The Dirichlet-multinomial GLM and likelihood-ratio test are unchanged; only the language and inference engine differ. Amendment v5.4.3. Source in src/leafcutter_ds/.

gotchas.symptom: MHCnuggets 2.4.0 failed at import with Keras 3 compatibility errors on newer TensorFlow versions.
gotchas.root_cause: MHCnuggets pins older Keras 2 API surface; TensorFlow greater than 2.15 defaults to Keras 3.
gotchas.fix: Patched three MHCnuggets source files for Keras 3 compatibility (import path adjustments and model.compile signature updates). Patches documented in environment/mhcnuggets_keras3_patch.md and applied via a post-install script.

gotchas.symptom: arcasHLA 0.5.0 initial runs on Riaz samples produced sparse class II HLA typings, with only DRB1 successfully typed and DQ, DP loci returning empty results.
gotchas.root_cause: IMGTHLA database version older than 3.43.0 lacked adequate class II reference sequences for the Kallisto pseudoalignment step.
gotchas.fix: Upgraded to IMGTHLA 3.43.0. Amendment recorded in v5.4.2 sample-list validation.

gotchas.symptom: Bisbee installation failed on Colab Pro with dependency conflicts that could not be resolved within the environment.
gotchas.root_cause: Bisbee's dependency set conflicts with the pinned TensorFlow and pyro versions needed elsewhere in the pipeline.
gotchas.fix: Locked Mann-Whitney U as the orthogonal validation substitute with concordance threshold of 80 percent preserved from the original Bisbee pre-registration. Amendment v5.4.7.

gotchas.symptom: MSigDB Hallmark 2020 library queries returned HTTP 429 rate-limit errors during pathway enrichment.
gotchas.root_cause: Enrichr API rate-limits high-frequency queries against MSigDB libraries.
gotchas.fix: MSigDB Hallmark 2020 deferred and excluded from primary enrichment output. GO BP 2023, KEGG 2021 Human, and Reactome 2022 completed successfully. Documented as a caveat in Supplementary Figure S5 legend.

gotchas.symptom: Intermediate output files occasionally failed to write to Google Drive after long-running Colab sessions.
gotchas.root_cause: Colab-Drive I/O timeouts during high-throughput write operations; not systematic.
gotchas.fix: Locked 30-second retry with exponential backoff for all critical intermediate outputs, and staged outputs to Colab local disk first with a Drive-sync verification step.

gotchas.symptom: Junctions from LeafCutter did not match GENCODE annotated intron coordinates on initial junction-to-transcript mapping.
gotchas.root_cause: LeafCutter uses 1-based inclusive coordinates for intron ends; GENCODE uses 0-based half-open BED convention. Standard LeafCutter output has a plus 9 on 5-prime and minus 9 on 3-prime offset relative to the annotation.
gotchas.fix: Applied plus 9 / minus 9 coordinate offset after verification on a 15-intron known-biology diagnostic set (NUMB, TRPM1, CITED1, CNIH4, MBNL1, FCGR2A, SMIM14, ABCB7, STRN3 all matched exactly). Documented in Supplementary Figure S1 legend.

gotchas.symptom: STAR ReadsPerGene.out.tab column selection ambiguous between unstranded and reverse-stranded columns across cohorts.
gotchas.root_cause: Different cohorts used different library preparation kits with different strandedness conventions.
gotchas.fix: Locked strandedness inference per cohort based on splice-donor GT ratio in SJ.out.tab: Hugo and Riaz reverse-stranded; Gide reverse-stranded. Verified before Stage A2.

gotchas.symptom: Long-running Stage A alignment tasks (per-sample STAR + arcasHLA) exceeded Colab's continuous-session limit (approximately 12 hours) for the Gide cohort batch.
gotchas.root_cause: Colab Pro imposes a soft session limit that terminates long idle or continuous-compute tasks.
gotchas.fix: Batched Stage A alignment into per-cohort segments with checkpoint save-and-resume. Each cohort's STAR outputs verified for completeness before proceeding to the next stage.
